mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 7.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts above 850k units signal robust consumer demand and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlook.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of periods when HOUST exceeds 850k units.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 98.5 indicates weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite declining INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in CPI inflation below 2.0% signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through liquidity injections.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of periods when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightness regime (UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS growth > 150k) generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through wage inflation pressures and sectoral reflation.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of periods when both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A manufacturing recovery (INDPRO > 99.0 and HOUST > 850k) signals improved supply-side conditions that reduce input costs and boost equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 35% of periods when both INDPRO > 99.0 and HOUST > 850k.
